complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the MCP server foundation setup into specific implementation steps including: TypeScript project initialization with MCP SDK dependencies, server class implementation with proper initialization and lifecycle management, tool registration framework design, communication protocol setup, error handling and logging infrastructure, configuration management for Claude Desktop integration, and comprehensive testing setup for both unit and integration tests.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium-high complexity due to MCP protocol specifics and Claude Desktop integration requirements. Need to establish robust foundation with proper architecture, error handling, and testing infrastructure. Multiple distinct components require separate implementation focus.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Decompose PDF processing into: PyMuPDF/pdfplumber setup and configuration, text extraction with layout preservation, multi-column layout handling, academic formatting detection (footnotes, references, equations), metadata extraction system, Document class design with hierarchical structure, encoding and special character handling, publisher-specific format handlers for IEEE/ACM/Springer patterns, and comprehensive testing framework with diverse academic PDF samples.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to varied academic PDF formats, complex layouts, and need for multiple fallback strategies. Requires handling edge cases from different publishers and maintaining structural integrity. Critical foundation component affecting all downstream features.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Structure semantic chunking implementation into: spaCy NLP pipeline setup for academic text, paragraph-level preservation logic, argumentative unit detection using semantic analysis, sliding window implementation with configurable overlap, natural breakpoint identification algorithms, TextSegment class with relationship management, coherence scoring system development, and academic writing pattern recognition for citations and cross-references.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Complex due to need to preserve semantic meaning and argumentative flow in academic texts. Requires sophisticated NLP understanding and careful balance between chunk size and context preservation. Critical for search quality.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Organize embedding system into: sentence-transformers setup with model selection (SciBERT vs fine-tuned models), domain-specific optimization for humanities/social sciences terminology, embedding pipeline implementation with batch processing, FAISS vector store integration and indexing, confidence scoring mechanism for embedding quality, and performance optimization for large-scale embedding generation.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Moderate complexity focused on model selection and optimization rather than complex implementation. Main challenges are choosing appropriate models and tuning for academic domain. Well-established libraries reduce implementation complexity.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Design search system with: hybrid search architecture combining semantic and keyword matching, query preprocessing and expansion for academic terms, FAISS integration for vector similarity search, result ranking algorithm with relevance scoring, context preservation system for paragraph-level results, confidence indicator implementation, caching layer for performance optimization, and natural language query parser for academic queries.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Complex due to need for hybrid approach and sophisticated ranking. Requires balancing multiple search strategies and ensuring context preservation. Performance requirements add additional complexity for optimization.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Implement citation system with: pybtex/citeproc-py integration for citation parsing, page/paragraph mapping system with exact location tracking, bibliographic metadata extraction from PDFs, Citation class with multi-format support (APA/MLA/Chicago), validation system ensuring 100% accuracy, export functionality for BibTeX/Zotero/Mendeley, source attribution tracking for all segments, format conversion utilities, and zero-tolerance testing framework for citation errors.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: High complexity due to zero-tolerance accuracy requirement and multiple citation format support. Requires precise tracking and validation systems. Critical for academic credibility with no room for errors.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Create MCP tools including: literature_search tool with parameter validation, document_retrieval tool with streaming support, citation_lookup tool implementation, source_attribution tool design, structured response format specifications for Claude compatibility, error handling and validation framework, concurrent request management system, and comprehensive integration testing with Claude Desktop.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Moderate complexity as it primarily involves integrating existing functionality into MCP format. Main challenges are proper error handling, response formatting, and ensuring smooth Claude Desktop interaction.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Build document intelligence with: DocumentStructure class for hierarchical navigation, cross-reference detection using NLP patterns, resolution algorithm for 'see Chapter X' references, footnote/endnote extraction and linking system, terminology mapping for author-specific language, confidence scoring for resolved references, academic document convention handlers, and structural extraction for various document types.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Complex due to varied reference patterns and need for intelligent resolution. Requires sophisticated NLP and pattern matching. Academic documents have diverse structures requiring flexible handling.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Develop synthesis capabilities with: ArgumentTracker class for concept evolution tracking, chronological development analysis across authors, ContradictionDetector using semantic similarity and sentiment analysis, nuance detection for subtle disagreements, ResearchGapAnalyzer with methodology pattern recognition, coverage analysis algorithms, synthesis validation with coherence metrics, multi-document narrative construction framework, attribution management system, and confidence scoring for synthesis quality assessment.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Very high complexity requiring advanced NLP and semantic understanding. Must handle nuanced academic arguments and identify subtle patterns across multiple sources. Most intellectually challenging component requiring sophisticated algorithms.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Implement production features including: multi-layer caching strategy for embeddings and results, memory optimization with lazy loading patterns, batch processing system for systematic reviews, comprehensive error handling and recovery mechanisms, monitoring and metrics collection infrastructure, performance profiling and optimization for <3s response time, configuration management system, and deployment automation scripts.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Complex due to multiple optimization targets and production requirements. Requires careful profiling and optimization across entire system. Critical for usability with large document libraries.
